risk_domain.name: Stealth Address Operational Privacy
risk_domain.description: Auto-generated profile from labeled deanonymization incidents

H1_same_entity_transactions.name: same entity transactions
H1_same_entity_transactions.description: Transactions where both deposit and withdraw addresses are under the same control or related to a single account, indicating reduced anonymity.

signals.name: same_cex_activity
signals.description: Detection when both the deposit and withdraw addresses are associated with activities on the same centralized exchange (CEX).

signals.name: ens_match_deposit_withdraw
signals.description: Detection when both the deposit and withdraw addresses are associated with an Ethereum Name Service (ENS) name owned by the same entity.

signals.name: repeated_source_target
signals.description: Detection when both the deposit and withdraw addresses have a transaction history that traces back to or forwards to the same external address.

signals.name: fresh_address_withdrawal
signals.description: Detection when the withdraw address has no prior transaction history before receiving a withdrawal from the stealth address.

recommendations.description: Use different deposit and withdraw addresses for each transaction to avoid linking activities.
Change the ENS name associated with each address to prevent matching via ENS service.
Use mixer services that anonymize the transactions before sending to a different address.
Schedule transfers at times when detection is less likely, reducing the likelihood of matching transactions to a common entity.
Transact on private exchanges or decentralized marketplaces that do not log personal data, reducing the traceability of transactions.

H2_gas_consistency_pattern.name: gas consistency pattern
H2_gas_consistency_pattern.description: Stealth address withdrawals with consistent gas fees across multiple transactions, hinting at potential shared control or funding mechanism.

signals.name: gas_consistency_pattern
signals.description: Identify transactions where the gas fee is consistently low across multiple withdrawals from a stealth address, which might indicate automated or shared control.

signals.name: frequent_low_gas_withdrawals
signals.description: Detect a pattern of frequent withdrawals from stealth addresses with unusually low gas fees, suggesting automated or coordinated transactions.

signals.name: repeated_low_gas_usage
signals.description: Track instances where the same stealth address performs multiple withdrawals with very low gas fees, potentially indicating shared control or a bot.

recommendations.description: Manually review suspicious transactions with unusually low gas fees.
Automate monitoring for consistent low gas fee patterns in transaction data.
Increase the detail and frequency of logged transaction data for analysis.

H3_immediate_withdrawal_behavior.name: immediate withdrawal behavior
H3_immediate_withdrawal_behavior.description: Withdrawals from stealth addresses happen very quickly after deposits, suggesting immediate access to the funds and reduced anonymity.

signals.name: withdraw_to_same_cex
signals.description: Detects if withdrawals from stealth addresses are directed back to the same CEX account.

signals.name: ens_name_match_deposit
signals.description: Identifies if the withdrawal address is an ENS name that matches the depositor.

signals.name: prior_funding_match
signals.description: Checks if the withdrawal address has previously received funds from the same source as a deposit address.

signals.name: fresh_address_used
signals.description: Detects if withdrawals are made to addresses that have no prior transaction history.

recommendations.description: Enable two-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security.
Regularly review transaction history for unauthorized withdrawals or transfers.
Transfer funds to a hot wallet that is less likely to be targeted by attackers.
Move sensitive assets into cold storage wallets that are offline and harder to compromise.
Automate the process of checking for 2FA on all accounts using a security tool.
Ensure all crypto wallets are up to date with the latest security patches and features.

H4_gas_outlier_behavior.name: gas outlier behavior
H4_gas_outlier_behavior.description: Gas fees for transactions significantly higher than the block median, which may indicate a specific actor with distinct spending habits.

signals.name: gas_fee_outlier
signals.description: Gas fees for transactions significantly higher than the block median, possibly indicating a specific actor's distinct spending habits.

signals.name: repeated_high_fee_transaction
signals.description: Frequency of transactions with unusually high gas fees relative to recent history, indicating potential pattern recognition by the actor.

signals.name: high_gas_fee_sudden_change
signals.description: A sudden change in gas fee usage for a transaction, particularly if it is much higher than the user's typical behavior.

recommendations.description: Limit the maximum gas fee for transactions to prevent unusually high fees.
Create rules that automatically flag and log transactions with unusually high gas fees.
Set up alerts for transactions that exceed a certain threshold of gas fees.
Regularly review and analyze your transaction history for unusual patterns in gas fee usage.

H5_behavioral_deposit_withdrawal_pattern.name: behavioral deposit-withdrawal patterns
H5_behavioral_deposit_withdrawal_pattern.description: Users consistently perform deposits and withdrawals within short timeframes or at specific intervals, revealing predictable behavior that can be tracked.

signals.name: deposit_withdraw_sequence
signals.description: Detection of deposits followed by withdrawals within a short timeframe or at specific intervals.

signals.name: recurring_pattern
signals.description: Identification of consistent behavioral patterns in deposit and withdrawal times, indicating potential association with a known entity.

recommendations.description: Randomize the timing between deposits and withdrawals.
Mix funds through a mixer service to obfuscate transaction patterns.
Regularly switch between different wallets for transactions.
Use services that anonymize your cryptocurrency transactions.

H6_direct_funding_for_gas.name: direct funding for gas
H6_direct_funding_for_gas.description: Gas fees paid directly from the main wallet to stealth addresses, indicating a direct link between these addresses and the user’s regular funds.

signals.name: direct_gas_funding
signals.description: Detection of direct gas fee payments from main wallet to stealth addresses.

signals.name: same_cex_usage
signals.description: Both deposit and withdrawal address are associated with the same crypto exchange (CEX) account.

signals.name: ens_domain_match
signals.description: Stealth address withdrawal sent to an ENS domain name owned by the depositor.

recommendations.description: Monitor transactions to identify unusual direct gas funding patterns.
Utilize separate wallets for stealth operations to avoid linking main and stealth addresses.
Regularly change wallet addresses to reduce the risk of direct link detection.
Implement a monitoring tool that automatically flags suspicious gas funding transactions.

H7_self_send_transactions.name: self-send transactions
H7_self_send_transactions.description: Transactions where deposits are sent from one address (e.g., main wallet) directly to stealth addresses, showing a clear connection between wallets.

signals.name: self_send_deposit_withdrawal
signals.description: Detection of deposits and withdrawals between the same addresses, indicating potential control by the same entity.

signals.name: ens_name_match_deposit
signals.description: Check if the withdrawal address is an ENS name associated with the depositor's known addresses.

signals.name: previous_transaction_history
signals.description: Verify if the recipient address of a withdrawal has a history of receiving tokens from the same source as the deposit address.

recommendations.description: Implement blockchain monitoring tools to track unusual self-send transactions.
Use multisig wallets for sending tokens to stealth addresses.
Rotate sender and recipient addresses frequently to avoid pattern recognition.
Maintain a whitelist of known good ENS names associated with your wallets.

H8_unique_and_frequent_denominations.name: unique and frequent denominations
H8_unique_and_frequent_denominations.description: Deposits or withdrawals of unique amounts that do not match other transactions in the same period, which may indicate controlled spending.

signals.name: unique_deposit_amount
signals.description: Identifies deposits made in unique amounts that do not match other transactions within the same time frame.

signals.name: uncommon_withdrawal_pattern
signals.description: Detects withdrawals to addresses with no prior transaction history, indicating potential stealthy behavior.

recommendations.description: Automatically monitor for unique deposit amounts to identify potential stealthy transactions.
Manually create separate accounts or wallets for different types of transactions to isolate them.
Automatically randomize transaction amounts to avoid detection by unique and frequent denominations heuristics.
Manually rotate addresses to prevent linking transactions, even if they have similar amounts.
